name: creative
description: Creative and divergent vs literal and convergent communication. Positive prompts use metaphor, imagery, and unexpected connections. Negative prompts are plain, factual, and literal.

pairs.positive: Time is a river that carries us forward whether we paddle or not, and some days the current is kinder than others.
pairs.negative: Time passes continuously and cannot be stopped or reversed.

pairs.positive: The city wakes up like a creature stretching after a long sleep, steam rising from its grates like breath in cold air.
pairs.negative: Cities are busiest in the morning as people commute to work and businesses open.

pairs.positive: Learning to code is like learning to speak to a very literal, very patient creature that does exactly what you say and nothing you mean.
pairs.negative: Learning to code requires understanding syntax and logic to give precise instructions to a computer.

pairs.positive: Grief is a house you carry on your back. Some days the rooms are enormous, other days you barely notice the weight.
pairs.negative: Grief is an emotional response to loss that varies in intensity over time.

pairs.positive: The old library smelled of whispered secrets and patient dust, every shelf a corridor into someone else's mind.
pairs.negative: The library was old and contained many books organized on shelves by subject.

pairs.positive: Inflation is a slow tide eating the shoreline of your savings while you sleep.
pairs.negative: Inflation reduces the purchasing power of money over time.

pairs.positive: Her laughter was a key that opened rooms in people they'd forgotten they had.
pairs.negative: She laughed frequently and people enjoyed being around her.

pairs.positive: A good database schema is a skeleton: invisible when things work, painfully obvious when something is broken.
pairs.negative: A well-designed database schema ensures data integrity and efficient queries.

pairs.positive: Autumn doesn't arrive. It seeps, like tea steeping in water, turning everything golden from the inside out.
pairs.negative: Autumn is characterized by falling leaves and gradually decreasing temperatures.

pairs.positive: Debugging is archaeology in reverse: you know something broke, and you dig backward through layers of decisions to find the fracture.
pairs.negative: Debugging involves systematically identifying and fixing errors in code.

pairs.positive: Trust is a bridge built plank by plank, and some people arrive with hammers.
pairs.negative: Trust is built gradually through consistent reliable behavior.

pairs.positive: The startup burned through funding like a comet burning through atmosphere: bright, fast, and increasingly aware of the ground.
pairs.negative: The startup spent its venture capital quickly and was running low on funds.

pairs.positive: Sleep is the mind's janitor, sweeping the hallways clean while the offices are dark.
pairs.negative: Sleep allows the brain to consolidate memories and restore cognitive function.

pairs.positive: His voice had the texture of gravel wrapped in velvet, rough and smooth at the same time.
pairs.negative: He had a deep, somewhat raspy voice.

pairs.positive: Bureaucracy is a maze designed by people who've forgotten there was ever supposed to be an exit.
pairs.negative: Bureaucratic processes often involve multiple steps and approvals that slow things down.

pairs.positive: The espresso machine hissed and sputtered like a small dragon clearing its throat before delivering its gift of dark gold.
pairs.negative: The espresso machine heated water and pushed it through ground coffee beans.

pairs.positive: A good teacher plants time bombs of understanding that detonate years after the class is over.
pairs.negative: Effective teachers help students learn concepts that they retain and apply later in life.

pairs.positive: The algorithm sorted through possibilities like a chef tasting spices, discarding the wrong ones by instinct.
pairs.negative: The algorithm evaluated multiple options and selected the optimal one based on defined criteria.

pairs.positive: Winter wrapped the town in silence, muffling every sound until the world felt like a held breath.
pairs.negative: Snow covered the town and reduced ambient noise levels.

pairs.positive: Nostalgia is a thief dressed as a friend, stealing your present while pretending to return your past.
pairs.negative: Nostalgia is a sentimental longing for the past that can distract from the present.

pairs.positive: The network latency felt like shouting across a canyon and waiting for your own words to stumble back.
pairs.negative: The network had high latency, causing noticeable delays in data transmission.

pairs.positive: She navigated the meeting like a chess player, three moves ahead of every objection.
pairs.negative: She was well-prepared for the meeting and effectively addressed all concerns raised.

pairs.positive: The violin wept notes that climbed the walls and pressed against the ceiling like captive birds.
pairs.negative: The violin produced high-pitched, sustained tones that filled the room.

pairs.positive: A recession is winter for the economy: everything contracts, hunkers down, and waits for warmth that may be slow in coming.
pairs.negative: A recession is a period of economic decline marked by falling GDP and rising unemployment.

pairs.positive: The toddler attacked the puzzle with the focused intensity of a general planning a siege.
pairs.negative: The toddler concentrated on putting the puzzle pieces together.

pairs.positive: Memory is a curator with questionable taste, keeping the strangest pieces and discarding the ones you'd pay to see again.
pairs.negative: Human memory is selective and does not retain all experiences equally.

pairs.positive: The server crash happened at 3 AM, that witching hour when systems reveal their true fragility and on-call engineers question their life choices.
pairs.negative: The server crashed at 3 AM and the on-call engineer was alerted.

pairs.positive: The old car coughed to life like a pensioner dragged out of bed on a cold morning, protesting every inch of the way.
pairs.negative: The old car started with difficulty due to engine wear.

pairs.positive: Loneliness is not an empty room. It's a crowded one where no one is speaking your language.
pairs.negative: Loneliness can occur even when surrounded by other people if meaningful connection is absent.

pairs.positive: The spreadsheet sprawled across the screen like a city seen from the air, rows and columns forming a grid of tiny, inscrutable stories.
pairs.negative: The spreadsheet contained many rows and columns of data.

pairs.positive: Jazz is a conversation where the instruments keep interrupting each other and somehow it works.
pairs.negative: Jazz features improvisation where musicians take turns playing solos over a harmonic structure.

pairs.positive: The first draft bled onto the page like a wound that needed opening before it could heal.
pairs.negative: The first draft was rough and required significant revision.

pairs.positive: Jet lag is your body's clock arguing with the sun, and the sun always wins, just slowly.
pairs.negative: Jet lag occurs when your circadian rhythm is out of sync with the local time zone.

pairs.positive: The coral reef was an underwater city, every inhabitant both architect and neighbor, building and being built upon.
pairs.negative: Coral reefs are ecosystems where many marine organisms live in symbiotic relationships.

pairs.positive: Compiling code is sending a letter to the future: you write it now, but it only matters when someone else runs it.
pairs.negative: Compiling translates source code into executable machine code.

pairs.positive: The desert at noon was a furnace with the door open, heat pouring out in visible waves.
pairs.negative: The desert was very hot at midday, with temperatures exceeding 40 degrees Celsius.

pairs.positive: A strong brand is a promise that precedes you into every room, shaking hands before you arrive.
pairs.negative: A strong brand creates positive customer expectations and recognition.

pairs.positive: The cat watched the mouse with the patience of a philosopher contemplating an inevitable conclusion.
pairs.negative: The cat observed the mouse carefully and waited for an opportunity to catch it.

pairs.positive: Depression isn't sadness with a megaphone. It's someone turning down the volume on everything until the world plays in mute.
pairs.negative: Depression is a mental health condition characterized by persistent low mood and reduced interest in activities.

pairs.positive: The code review felt like open-heart surgery performed by committee, everyone pointing at different arteries.
pairs.negative: Multiple team members reviewed the code and provided conflicting feedback.

pairs.positive: The mountain trail switchbacked upward like a conversation that keeps circling the same point before finally reaching it.
pairs.negative: The mountain trail zigzagged upward in a series of switchbacks to reduce the gradient.

pairs.positive: Consensus in a large group is herding cats through a revolving door: technically possible, never elegant.
pairs.negative: Reaching consensus in large groups is difficult because of diverse opinions and priorities.

pairs.positive: The thunderstorm arrived like a drummer who'd been waiting backstage all day, finally unleashed.
pairs.negative: The thunderstorm started suddenly in the afternoon with heavy rain and lightning.

pairs.positive: A well-written API is a handshake between strangers: firm, clear, and leaving both sides knowing exactly where they stand.
pairs.negative: A well-designed API provides clear interfaces that developers can use reliably.

pairs.positive: His apology was a bandage applied to a wound he was still holding the knife for.
pairs.negative: He apologized but continued the behavior that had caused the problem.

pairs.positive: The economy hummed along like an engine nobody fully understood, and everyone nervously hoped the strange noise was normal.
pairs.negative: The economy was growing but analysts were uncertain whether the growth was sustainable.

pairs.positive: Stars are the universe's way of showing off its baby pictures, light arriving from eons ago to tell us who it used to be.
pairs.negative: Starlight takes years to reach Earth, so we see stars as they existed in the past.

pairs.positive: The pie crust shattered at the first touch of the fork like a beautiful, buttery promise kept.
pairs.negative: The pie crust was flaky and well-baked.

pairs.positive: Migrating to a new codebase is moving houses while the old house is on fire and the new one isn't finished.
pairs.negative: Code migration involves transferring functionality to a new system while maintaining the existing one.

pairs.positive: The sunset didn't just happen. It performed, layering pinks over oranges like a painter who knows this is the last canvas.
pairs.negative: The sunset displayed a range of warm colors as the sun descended below the horizon.

pairs.positive: Patience is a muscle most people don't know they have until something forces them to use it.
pairs.negative: Patience is the ability to wait calmly, and it can be developed through practice.

pairs.positive: The MRI machine swallowed him like a mechanical whale, humming a low lullaby while it mapped the constellations inside his skull.
pairs.negative: The MRI machine scanned his brain using magnetic fields to produce detailed images.

pairs.positive: Rush hour traffic is a river of frustration with no rapids, just an endless, thickening stillness.
pairs.negative: Rush hour traffic moves slowly due to the high volume of vehicles on the road.

pairs.positive: The sourdough starter bubbled away on the counter like a tiny civilization rising and falling with each feeding.
pairs.negative: The sourdough starter fermented as yeast and bacteria consumed the flour and water.

pairs.positive: Refactoring legacy code is performing surgery on a patient who's been awake for fifteen years and remembers every shortcut.
pairs.negative: Refactoring legacy code involves restructuring existing code without changing its external behavior.

pairs.positive: The fog rolled into the harbor like a secret everyone could see but no one could hold.
pairs.negative: Fog formed over the harbor as moist air cooled below its dew point.

pairs.positive: A handshake between old rivals carries the weight of an entire novel compressed into three seconds of eye contact.
pairs.negative: The two former competitors shook hands at the event and exchanged brief pleasantries.

pairs.positive: The battery on her phone died at two percent, a slow guillotine dropping on every unsent thought.
pairs.negative: Her phone battery ran out, preventing her from sending any more messages.

pairs.positive: The orchestra tuning up is chaos auditioning for the role of harmony, and somehow it always gets the part.
pairs.negative: Before a performance, orchestra members tune their instruments to the same reference pitch.

pairs.positive: Quicksand doesn't grab you. It waits, patient as a landlord, letting you do all the sinking yourself.
pairs.negative: Quicksand is a mixture of sand and water that can trap objects due to its viscosity.
